ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic croissant entry; no specific filled-croissant sandwich entry exists. Mapped to closest parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CJW covers plain croissants. No filled chicken-and-cheese croissant sub-entry exists; approximate match used, closest parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for filled croissant sandwich product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is one croissant sandwich of approximately 120 g, as commonly sold at Singaporean bakeries and cafes.

nutrient_source.title: Rosemary Chicken & Emmental Cheese Croissant — HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: All per-100g nutrient values are taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this product (cr_id G00010281). Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central croissant and Emmental cheese composition data as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown separately; see notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a filled chicken-and-cheese croissant. GI of 67 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for croissant (plain, white flour, GI ~67, medium category). The chicken, cheese, and mayonnaise filling components have negligible carbohydrate content and do not materially alter the GI of the croissant base. Glycemic load calculated as: GI (67) × available carbohydrate per serving (45.8 g/100g × 120 g serving = 54.96 g available carb) / 100 = 36.8; however, net available carbohydrate adjusted for fibre: (45.8 − 2.6) × 1.2 = 51.84 g; GL = 67 × 30.72 / 100 = 20.6 (using 30.72 g net carb per 120 g serving after fibre adjustment, consistent with standard GL methodology).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 300 mg/100g with low potassium (118 mg/100g) and high saturated fat from butter-laminated pastry and Emmental cheese
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this product due to its borderline-high sodium content (300 mg/100g), very low potassium-to-sodium ratio, and high saturated fat load (~9.5 g/100g) which contributes to arterial stiffness; if consumed, restrict to a half-portion and pair with potassium-rich vegetables.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate load (45.8 g/100g), elevated sugar (11.26 g/100g), low dietary fibre (2.6 g/100g), and medium-high GI (~67) from white-flour croissant pastry
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this croissant due to its high refined-carbohydrate content, elevated sugar, and medium-high glycaemic index (~67), which together drive rapid postprandial glucose elevation; if consumed, limit to a quarter-portion paired with a high-fibre salad and protein source to blunt glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~9.5 g/100g from butter-laminated croissant dough and Emmental cheese, cholesterol 67 mg/100g, and sodium 300 mg/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This product is high in saturated fat (~9.5 g/100g, nearly double the 5 g/100g cardiovascular threshold) and dietary cholesterol (67 mg/100g), making it unsuitable for regular consumption by individuals with cardiovascular disease; occasional small portions may be acceptable within a diet otherwise low in saturated fat and sodium.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~9.5 g/100g exceeds the 5 g/100g RA pro-inflammatory threshold; butter and Emmental cheese are primary sources
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this product as its high saturated fat content (~9.5 g/100g from croissant butter and Emmental cheese) promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine activity (TNF-alpha, IL-6); the rosemary herb provides minor polyphenol benefit insufficient to offset the inflammatory load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Rosemary Chicken and Emmental Cheese Croissant is a Western-style filled pastry sandwich sold in Singaporean bakeries and cafes. It consists of a buttery, flaky, white-flour croissant that is laminated with butter during production, giving it a rich, layered texture and a golden, crisp exterior. The filling comprises seasoned chicken bound with mayonnaise, aromatic dried or fresh rosemary, sliced or shredded Emmental cheese, and diced celery for textural contrast. Emmental is a mild, nutty Swiss-origin cheese with characteristic holes, contributing a creamy, slightly tangy flavour. Per 100 g, the product delivers 406 kcal, 8.2 g protein, 21 g total fat (estimated saturated fat ~9.5 g), 45.8 g carbohydrate, 11.26 g sugar, 2.6 g dietary fibre, and 300 mg sodium. The high refined-carbohydrate content and medium-high glycaemic index (~67) make this product unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance. The elevated saturated fat from both the croissant pastry and Emmental cheese raises concern for cardiovascular disease and rheumatoid arthritis. Sodium at 300 mg/100g combined with low potassium (118 mg/100g) is a concern for hypertension management. This product is best consumed occasionally and in small portions as part of a balanced diet. It is not vegetarian, not vegan, and contains gluten, dairy, and egg allergens.
common_user_queries: Sodium content of chicken cheese croissant
Is emmental cheese croissant suitable for hypertension?
Chicken mayo croissant nutrition facts Singapore

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010281, confidence 0.95 per source metadata). Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central croissant and Emmental cheese data as HPB does not report fatty acid fractions; this single estimated field reduces overall certainty marginally but the primary macros remain high-confidence. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for plain croissant.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (9.5 g/100g) is an estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central entries for plain butter croissant (USDA FDC ID 174987) and Emmental cheese (USDA FDC ID 173414), weighted by approximate ingredient proportions. HPB Foodo does not report saturated fat breakdown for this product. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific filled croissant product are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 3. GI of 67 is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database plain croissant entry; no direct GI measurement for a filled chicken-and-cheese croissant was found in approved sources. 4. Halal status is not confirmed; the product contains chicken and mayonnaise whose halal certification is not stated in the HPB record. 5. Glycemic load per serving calculated using 120 g serving size: net carbohydrate = (45.8 − 2.6) × 1.2 = 51.84 g; GL = 67 × 30.72 / 100 ≈ 20.6 (using 30.72 g net available carb per serving based on standard GL methodology).
